page: OVHcloud c2-15 is a KVM-based compute-optimized server instance. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) running at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4 dedicated physical cores with no hyperthreading, 15.0 GB of RAM providing 3.75 GB per core, and a bundled 100 GB local SSD. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate mixed performance profiles. The instance demonstrates average performance in Passmark CPU tests with a score of 6764.0 and Passmark database operations at 2629.78. Redis SET operations are also average at 5.89 million operations per second. However, raw CPU compute benchmarks show limitations, with stress-ng single-core performance falling into the bottom 10% at 872.67 and multi-core performance in the bottom 25% at 3481.67. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is weak at 36.82 GB/sec read and 23.61 GB/sec write, though small-block cached read bandwidth is average at 346.97 GB/sec. LLM inference speeds are average for small 135M models but poor for medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c2-15 offers dedicated physical cores and local SSD storage at a lower tier of the hardware spectrum, making it a viable option for workloads requiring predictable CPU allocation without the premium of modern high-performance architectures. Given its average database and Redis performance but weaker raw computational and memory bandwidth metrics, this server is best suited for light database operations, basic web serving, Redis caching, and general-purpose utility workloads.
description: OVHcloud c2-15 is a KVM-based compute-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Haswell architecture clocked at 3.0 GHz. It is equipped with 15.0 GB of RAM, a 100 GB local SSD, and one public IPv4 address. Benchmarks show average performance in database operations and Redis SET operations, alongside average small-block cached memory bandwidth. However, raw CPU performance is limited, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core benchmarks scoring in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. Large-block memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput are also weak. Qualitatively, the c2-15 provides dedicated CPU resources at an entry-level performance tier, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads that require dedicated cores without needing cutting-edge compute power. It is best suited for light database tasks, Redis caching, and general-purpose utility workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud c2-15 is a KVM-based server with 4 dedicated Intel Haswell cores, 15 GB RAM, and a 100 GB SSD, offering average database and Redis performance for entry-level dedicated workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud c2-15 is a KVM server with 4 dedicated Intel cores, 15 GB RAM, and a 100 GB SSD, suited for light database and caching workloads.
tagline: An entry-level dedicated server featuring four physical Intel cores and local SSD storage for predictable, budget-conscious utility workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated physical Intel cores with no hyperthreading clocked at 3.0 GHz
15.0 GB of RAM and 100 GB of local SSD storage
Average performance in Redis caching and Passmark database operations
Weaker raw CPU compute and large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks
Best suited for light database tasks, Redis caching, and general utility workloads
